claim.hypothesis: ...in value-based decision making with choice-contingent feedback, similarity-based generalization may cause the value of prospects near the boundary between good and bad outcomes to be permanently misestimated.
claim.statement: ...participants in the contingent-information condition had an average 1D score of .83, while those in the full-information condition had an average score of .75, CI  [.03, .14].

data.source: Experiment testing how different interventions connect to learning traps
data.access_details: Data from all experiments is available at https://osf.io/hrb3u/?view_onlyd374ae90615d4e5993ee1c502493673d
data.notes: Participants recruited from MTurk. Originally 101 participants, but 6 were excluded due to their inability to pass post-instruction quiz or for using external memory devices.

method.description: The authors ran an experiment, using cartoon bees as stimuli, to look at the effect of contingent feedback on learning traps and then analyzed the data using a Bayesian multi-level modeling approach.
method.steps: (1) Authors ran computer simulations to understand the counterfactuals related to learning models; (2) Recruit participants into the experiment through MTurk; (3) Participants played the role of beekeeper and tried to harvest honey for rewards, where some hives would give honey and others would not and then, based on the treatment they were allocated, they would accept or reject job applications and then receive feedback based on treatment; (4) After learning phase, there is a test phase that had the same acceptance structure, but no feedback; (5) Then participants filled out a post-experiment survey; (6) The authors constructed measures of the divergent behavior by taking the proportion of participants that were consistent with the task structure (2D) or with the learning trap (1D); (7) The authors analyzed the results using a Bayesian multi-level modeling approach.

results.summary: Participants in the contingent-information treatment had higher 1D scores on average than those in the full-information treatment.

metadata.original_paper_title: The Limits of Learning: Exploration, Generalization, and the Development of Learning Traps
